channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Arnold Palmer's daughter reacts to Donald Trump's references to her father
item.description: One of the late golf legend Arnold Palmer's daughters calls Donald Trump’s references to her father’s genitalia "a poor choice of approaches” to honoring his memory.
item.dc:creator: Meg Kinnard, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Harris won't support expanding fossil fuel drilling, campaign says
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris will not support an expansion to drilling, the campaign said, despite Harris touting record-setting domestic U.S. oil production under Biden.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Musk says he'll award $1M every day to Pennsylvania voters who sign his petition
item.description: Elon Musk announced he will pay up to $1 million per day to Pennsylvania voters who sign his America PAC petition leading up to Election Day. The petition asks signers to support free speech and the right to bear arms.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What Vice President Harris left out about Biden admin's role in border crisis: A timeline
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris faced a series of questions about her administration's handling of the crisis at the southern border – but she left out a lot.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: DOJ once OK'd law at center of Youngkin voter roll-culling order feds now suing to block
item.description: The state law at the center of an election security order Gov. Glenn Youngkin is now being sued by the feds over was previously given the DOJ's blessing.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Voting groups that got a boost from Harris' candidacy are still working to persuade voters of color
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Left-leaning voter engagement groups that saw a surge in support and energy after Vice President Kamala Harris replaced President Joe Biden as the Democrats' White House nominee are aligning with a key pillar of her campaign in the final stretch before Election Day — trying to turn
item.dc:creator: BY AYANNA ALEXANDER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump works the fry station and holds a drive-thru news conference at a Pennsylvania McDonald's
item.description: Republican presidential nominee Donald Trump has manned the fry station at a McDonald's in Pennsylvania before staging an impromptu news conference, answering questions from reporters through the drive-thru window.
item.dc:creator: Michelle L. Price, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Catholic bishops are donating millions less to defeat abortion this year. Here’s why
item.description: In recent years, Catholic bishops have spent millions on campaigns to defeat abortion rights at the ballot box. This year, they're taking a dramatically different approach.
item.dc:creator: Rosemary Westwood and Jack Jenkins, MPR News

item.title: Republicans and Democrats are both preparing for long legal battles over the 2024 election results
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Republican Donald Trump, who still refuses to accept that he lost the 2020 election to Democrat Joe Biden, says he wants a presidential victory Nov. 5 to be so overwhelming that the results are “too big to rig.” “We want a landslide," he recently told supporters in Georgia.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, COLLEEN LONG and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Harris urges Black churchgoers in Georgia to head out to vote and gets an assist from Stevie Wonder
item.description: Kamala Harris has visited two Atlanta-area church where she summoned Black members of the congregations to turn out at the polls.
item.dc:creator: Colleen Long, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump talks about Arnold Palmer's genitalia at Pennsylvania rally
item.description: Politicians saluting Palmer in his hometown is nothing new. Trump spent 12 full minutes doing so at the top of his speech in Latrobe on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE and WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: What to know about Republican challenges to overseas and military voting
item.description: In three swing states, GOP groups are suing to challenge the ballots cast by U.S. citizens living abroad, including military members. Many voting experts say these cases are likely to go nowhere.
item.dc:creator: Hansi Lo Wang, MPR News

item.title: The US is investigating an unauthorized release of classified documents on Israel's attack plans
item.description: The United States is investigating an unauthorized release of classified documents that assess Israel’s plans to attack Iran and note that Israel continues to move military assets in place to conduct a military strike in response to Iran’s blistering ballistic missile attack on Oct. 1.
item.dc:creator: Zeke Miller, Mike Balsalmo, Eric Tucker And Tara Copp, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Harris makes last-ditch appeal to disenchanted Republicans
item.description: Vice President Harris and her allies are making a last-ditch effort to peel off Republican support from former President Trump in the final days of the election.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, NewsNation

item.title: Child labor violations on the rise, problem could get worse: report
item.description: Between 2011 and 2020, the rate of injuries among workers under 18 nearly doubled, a recent report says.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: Judges punishing Jan. 6 rioters say they fear more political violence as Election Day nears
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Over the past four years, judges at Washington’s federal courthouse have punished hundreds of rioters who stormed the U.S. Capitol in an unprecedented assault on the nation's democracy.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Minimum wage on the ballot in these 5 states
item.description: The federal minimum wage hasn't changed since 2009 but voters in several states will have the chance to raise hourly wages at the state level.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: VP Harris to campaign with anti-Trump Republican Liz Cheney in key battleground states
item.description: Vice President Harris will campaign in several “blue wall" suburban battleground states next week where she will be joined by former Republican Rep. Liz Cheney.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hovde shines spotlight on Tammy Baldwin's Wall Street partner during Wisconsin debate
item.description: Eric Hovde hammered Sen. Baldwin for not disclosing her longtime partner's finances, pointing out her position as a Wall Street wealth adviser.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Together they've been in office more than 70 years. Now their races could help swing House control
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — One is the longest-serving woman in congressional history. The other is dean of the Republican delegation from California and the chairman of a subcommittee responsible for Pentagon spending. Together, they have more than seven decades of experience serving in Congress.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: States to election officials: Follow the law or face consequences
item.description: In at least five battleground states, officials have investigated, indicted and even jailed officials who tried to interfere with the vote count.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Five takeaways from a fiery Wisconsin Senate debate
item.description: Sen. Tammy Baldwin (D-Wis.) and Republican challenger Eric Hovde squared off on a debate stage Friday evening in Madison in a fiery exchange of attacks that showed how intense this race has become after largely flying under the radar for months.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Harris and Trump hold competing rallies in Michigan
item.description: While the two candidates have been crisscrossing the swing states for weeks, this is the first time they are literally crossing paths, with each of them holding events in the suburbs north of Detroit.
item.dc:creator: Don Gonyea, Asma Khalid, and Eric McDaniel, MPR News

item.title: Trump microphone cuts out for 20 minutes at Detroit rally
item.description: Trump was in the midst of speaking about his tariff policy at the event on Friday, about to call it “the most beautiful word in the dictionary,” before the microphone went down. Someone handed the former president another microphone to try, but it didn’t work either.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Latest: Trump and Harris are campaigning for votes in pivotal Michigan
item.description: With the Nov. 5 election fast approaching, Democrat Kamala Harris and Republican Donald Trump are setting their sights on the key battleground state of Michigan on Friday. The vice president began her day in Grand Rapids before holding events in Lansing and Oakland County, northwest of Detroit.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A failed mic leaves Donald Trump pacing the stage in silence for nearly 20 minutes
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — Donald Trump paced his rally stage in silence for nearly 20 minutes Friday night in Detroit after his microphone cut out.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Taylor Lorenz: Musk draws more political ire than Cuban because of X
item.description: Musk has ardently endorsed Donald Trump, while Mark Cuban and Taylor Swift have thrown support behind Harris.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Montana GOP candidate who could flip control of Senate nagged by claims he lied about bullet wound
item.description: A former Park Service ranger says U.S. Senate hopeful Tim Sheehy of Montana has been lying about a bullet wound that the candidate said came from fighting in Afghanistan.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Brown, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Judge unseals heavily redacted trove of evidence in Trump's 2020 election interference case
item.description: The judge overseeing Donald Trump's 2020 election interference case made public Friday a heavily redacted trove of documents that provide a small glimpse into the evidence prosecutors will present if the case ever goes to trial.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Politics Friday: The 2024 election will determine who presides in Minnesota courtrooms, remember there are judges on your ballot
item.description: There are about 100 races on Minnesota’s election ballots that won’t get that much attention, and they’re all about judgeships. MPR News politics editor and his guests talk about judicial elections and why they’re different here than in other states.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst, Matthew Alvarez, and Ellie Roth, MPR News

item.title: Mayor Johnson's chief of staff says school CEO and board kept 5th Floor in the dark
item.description: In an exclusive sitdown, Cristina Pacione-Zayas invited WGN to City Hall’s 5th floor to, in her words, “clear the air.
item.dc:creator: Tahman Bradley, WGN 9

item.title: Nebraska court allows voter registrations from people with felony convictions
item.description: Nebraskans with felony convictions may register to vote after the state's highest court ruled Wednesday that a top election official had no authority to deem a law restoring those rights unconstitutional.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Lisa Cole, Nevada Assembly District 4 candidate, discusses economic development, affordable housing, and education
item.description: Lisa Cole, a candidate for Nevada Assembly District 4, appeared on the 8 News Now Daily Voting Guide to discuss her platform.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: NJ lawmakers' fight against teen vaping continues with new bill
item.description: TRENTON, N.J. (PIX11) -- The fight against youth vaping continues in the Garden State as some Trenton lawmakers are trying to pass a law prohibiting the delivery of electronic smoking devices to people under 21, requiring someone over 21 to sign off on the delivery.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11

item.title: Election 2024: Races to watch in New Jersey
item.description: NEW JERSEY (PX11) -- It's been one of the most notable years for New Jersey politics in recent memory — indictments, upsets, and loss are just some themes of what has been a year that could shape the Garden State's political future for years to come.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11
